us_equities.prose: Stocks rebounded broadly after the U.S. and Iran signed an interim pact to wind down the war, lifting sentiment across indices with small-cap and large-cap names participating in the recovery.

cited_sources.title: U.S. Stocks Poised to Close Week on Strong Note

rates_and_dollar.prose: The dollar firmed and Treasury yields held steady after the Federal Reserve held rates unchanged in a unanimous decision at Kevin Warsh's first meeting as Fed chairman, while traders began pricing growing odds of a rate hike later in the year.

cited_sources.title: Treasury Yields, Dollar Rise on First Fed Move Under Warsh

commodities.prose: Oil prices fell sharply after Trump signed a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with Saudi tankers resuming passage through the strait signaling a meaningful easing of the supply disruption that had driven prices to multi-month highs. Gold eased as acute geopolitical fear receded alongside the Iran deal.

cited_sources.title: Oil Prices Fall to Lowest Since War Began After Trump Signs Iran Deal

volatility_breadth.prose: Implied volatility fell as fears over oil supply eased following the Iran deal and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, reducing the near-term geopolitical risk premium that had kept hedging demand elevated.

cited_sources.title: Gas prices fall below $4 per gallon as oil supply fears ease after Iran deal
